agent._help: Agents are the personas that execute tasks. Each agent has a role, goal, and backstory that shape how the LLM responds.
agent.role: A short label describing the agent's function, e.g. "Research Analyst" or "Content Writer".
agent.goal: What this agent is trying to accomplish. The LLM uses the goal to guide its output.
agent.backstory: Context about the agent's expertise and personality. Provides framing for the LLM's system prompt.
agent.provider: The LLM provider to use for this agent (e.g. Anthropic, OpenAI).
agent.model: The model to use within the chosen provider (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, gpt-4o).
agent.tools: External tools this agent can call during execution. Tools must be registered in Settings > Tools.
agent.temperature: Controls randomness. Lower values (0–0.3) are more deterministic; higher values (0.7–1.0) are more creative.
agent.max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens in the LLM response. Leave empty for the model's default.
agent.system_prompt: Override the auto-generated system prompt. Use this for precise control over agent behavior.

task._help: Tasks define units of work assigned to agents. Each task produces an output that downstream tasks can reference.
task.description: What the agent should do. Supports template variables: {{input.xxx}} for workflow inputs, {{tasks.taskId.output}} for upstream outputs.
task.expected_output: Describes what the output should look like. Helps the LLM produce structured, useful results.
task.output_key: The variable name for this task's output. Other tasks reference it via {{tasks.<output_key>.output}}.
task.human_review: When enabled, execution pauses after this task for human approval before continuing.
task.review_instructions: Instructions shown to the reviewer when this task's output is ready for review.

execution_strategy._help: Controls how tasks are executed relative to each other.
execution_strategy.sequential: Tasks run one at a time in the order they appear.
execution_strategy.parallel: All tasks run simultaneously. Use when tasks are independent.
execution_strategy.hierarchical: A manager agent coordinates task assignment and ordering automatically.
execution_strategy.custom: Define explicit dependencies between tasks for fine-grained control over execution order.

node_inspector._help: Configure the selected node's behavior, inputs, and outputs.
node_inspector.provider: The LLM provider for this node.
node_inspector.system_prompt: The system message sent to the LLM.
node_inspector.prompt_template: The user prompt template. Use {{portName}} to reference input port values.
node_inspector.tools: Tools available to this LLM node for function calling.
node_inspector.action: Which action to invoke on the connected tool.
node_inspector.input_mapping: Map node input ports to tool action parameters.
node_inspector.config_overrides: Override tool-level configuration for this specific node.

template_syntax.description: Template variables let tasks reference workflow inputs and outputs from other tasks.

examples.description: Reference the output of a task with output_key 'research'
